briefing_memo: Coradix Technology Consulting has received federal funding (total amount not disclosed in available records), but is currently suspended from all federal contracts by Public Services and Procurement Canada as of March 2024. An active RCMP investigation, ongoing since October 2023, is examining alleged misconduct in the Coradix-Dalian joint venture's CBSA contracts, including collusion, credential falsification, unauthorized markups, and pass-through arrangements with GC Strategies; the venture also failed Indigenous procurement compliance audits in March 2024. No remediation signals are documented, and the suspension remains in effect with stop-work orders issued and new bids barred. Recommendation: No funding decisions should proceed until the RCMP investigation concludes and suspension is lifted; existing contracts should remain frozen pending investigation outcomes and any required integrity reviews.

actions.title: Refer to RCMP Anti-Corruption Unit for coordination on ongoing investigation
actions.rationale: Multiple CRITICAL-severity articles report active RCMP investigation into alleged misconduct in CBSA ArriveCan contract, with Parliamentary testimony from October 2023 indicating ongoing scrutiny.

actions.title: Open Integrity Regime review for alleged contract non-compliance
actions.rationale: Contractor failed to meet terms of Indigenous procurement requirements per March 2024 Globe and Mail reporting, indicating potential material breach of federal contracting obligations.

actions.title: Suspend all active contracts and pause new procurements pending investigation outcome
actions.rationale: Active RCMP criminal investigation combined with Parliamentary findings and contractor suspension creates immediate reputational and fiduciary risk for continued federal engagement.
evidence: Multiple CRITICAL news items within 24 months

actions.title: Conduct enhanced due diligence audit of all historical contracts with this vendor
actions.rationale: Pattern of alleged misconduct spanning 2019-2024 with 11 CRITICAL-severity adverse signals warrants systematic review of all past dealings to identify scope of potential irregularities.
